THE MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, AND EMAIL IT TO JUDGE PRO TEM TOM COHEN AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM . Notice Of Motion, Motion, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Strike Or, In The Alternative, To Seal Portions Of Defendant Pg&Es Motion Papers
Off Calendar Jan-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. DOUGLAS CARLSON, Esq. (tel: 415-956-9567) for the Plaintiffs.
Atty. CATHERINE MEULEMANS, Esq. of Frost Brown Todd LLP (tel: 415-694-8665) for Defendant PG&E.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFFS DOUGLAS CARLSON, AND MAYA ZUBKOVSKAYA's Motion To Strike Or, In The Alternative, To Seal Portions Of Defendant Pg&Es Motion Papers.

Matter discussed. Counsel Meulemans states the parties have settled the case. Upon conclusion, the Court rules as follows:

The Court finds an overriding interest to seal the records as the parties have settled the case. The Court finds there is no counterveiling interest by the public at this time. The Court grants Plaintiffs' motion to seal.

Counsel Douglas Carlson is to prepare and submit an order which repeats the substantive portion of the court's ruling, and outlines the documents to be sealed with the date they were filed. Counsel shall also submit redacted versions of the documents to be sealed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/HEK)

MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Dec-05-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. DOUGLAS CARLSON, Esq. (tel: 415-956-9567) for Plaintiffs.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFFS DOUGLAS CARLSON, AND MAYA ZUBKOVSKAYA's MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS.

Both parties have informed the clerk during the check in process that they are contesting the tentative ruling. Plaintiff's counsel states plaintiff does not stipulate to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen. Upon calling the calendar, the Court notes Defendant's counsel is not present. The clerk states Counsel Catherine Meulemans checked in for the defendant, but she is no longer present on Zoom. Counsel Douglas Carlson states plaintiffs are contesting only the tentative ruling for the motion for relief from waiver of objections, and that he is now stipulating to the Judge Pro Tem. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: The subpoena shall be limited to: All documents and records, whether or not stored digitally and/or electronically, pertaining to the prescriptions of [drug 1] and [drug 2] to treat Maya V. Zubkovskaya, limited to, any itemized statement of the billing charges (including the date), medications and prescriptions. This limited request shall include any records related to the prescription of [drug 1] and [drug 2] held by Specialty Pharmacist, [name redacted]. Any reference to the prescribing physician(s) shall be redacted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, and email it to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com .

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. =(525/JPT)

MINI MINUTES:

Atty. DOUGLAS CARLSON, Esq. (tel: 415-956-9567) for Plaintiffs.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF MAYA ZUBKOVSKAYA's Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Objections.

Both parties have informed the clerk during the check in process that they are contesting the tentative ruling. Plaintiff's counsel states plaintiff does not stipulate to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen. Upon calling the calendar, the Court notes Defendant's counsel is not present. The clerk states Counsel Catherine Meulemans checked in for the defendant, but she is no longer present on Zoom. Counsel Douglas Carlson states plaintiffs are contesting only the tentative ruling for the motion for relief from waiver of objections, and that he is now stipulating to the Judge Pro Tem. Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court vacates its tentative ruling and grants Plaintiff Maya Zubkovskaya's Motion For Relief From Waiver of Objections.

Counsel Douglas Carlson is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, and email it to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com .
